heroCommand: umbrella launch "Research competitors and mint a strategy on Base"

mission.title: Market intelligence → Base strategy
mission.objective: Research top competitors, synthesize positioning, draft on-chain mint parameters.

heroLogs: [supervisor] Pre-flight: mission class = growth + chain touchpoint
[supervisor] Planner: emitting DAG (7 tasks, 3 parallel lanes)

toolActions.detail: Base · mint strategy capsule (value set on approve)

selfHealSnippet.fix: PATCH auth/middleware.ts — stale tenant cache invalidated

ceoBriefing:
• Competitors cluster on price; differentiation is **latency + custody** — own the "sovereign desk" narrative.
• Ship a **checkpointed** Base mint path: policy gate at 7/10, human signature above 7, integrity hash on snapshots.
• Next 14 days: one public case study (redacted) + DR drill with restore token handshake.

capabilities.title: Planner → DAG
capabilities.summary: Decomposes a mission into a checkpointed DAG with parallel lanes and explicit policy gates.

capabilities.title: Self-heal loop
capabilities.summary: Bounded retries with AUDITOR verify; every FILE_WRITE is followed by a re-run or revert.

capabilities.title: Risk governance
capabilities.summary: Every tool call has a 1–10 risk score; threshold is user-owned, blocks propose-only above the line.

capabilities.title: Swarm orchestration
capabilities.summary: Scraper / coder / auditor / supervisor workers coordinated by a single supervisor with shared DAG.

capabilities.title: Integrity & DR
capabilities.summary: Snapshots per checkpoint, content-addressed, with a GET /v1/health/dr surface for trust UX.

capabilities.title: Chain-aware actions
capabilities.summary: Wallet proposals are staged — never auto-submitted above threshold. Signature is always yours.

faq.q: Why Umbrella if I already use Cursor / Claude Code?
faq.a: Cursor is a reactive editor. Umbrella is a long-horizon runner: master task, sub-objectives, policy gates, and checkpoints. It's a different job.
Hosted inference is tenant-isolated and bounded by explicit tool permissions. Local/BYO-compute modes are on the roadmap for users who need it.

faq.q: Do I need tokens to use it?
faq.a: No. Stripe covers the normal path. UMBRELLA top-ups are optional and only touch a narrow, documented surface.

faq.q: How do you stop runaway loops?
faq.a: Per-user daily caps, per-task token budgets, and a policy threshold that blocks high-risk tool calls until you approve.
